header_summary:
In recent discussions, the Bitcoin development community has extensively explored enhancing transaction privacy, emphasizing the importance of community input and collaboration. An informative thread on [DelvingBitcoin.org](https://delvingbitcoin.org/t/state-of-the-transaction-privacy-work-in-bitcoin/2622) serves as a critical resource, underscoring efforts to address privacy concerns through broader engagement and diverse expert insights. This approach aims to deepen understanding and develop effective solutions within the cryptocurrency ecosystem.

Another focal point has been the integration of data-carrying annexes in P2TR transactions to supervise AI agents, ensuring accurate and verifiable responses under predefined constraints. This novel use of Bitcoin’s blockchain and scripting capabilities, discussed by the Bitcoin development community, could potentially reduce information asymmetry and align incentives across stakeholders, transforming global AI-driven computations. The discussion also touches on economic and cryptographic challenges, highlighting the necessity for robust, scalable solutions to make these applications viable and secure. [More details here](https://gnusha.org/pi/bitcoindev/CALZpt+GGORG3bgM0C3sQYVNbc1W7aFyb0qP_c2xbZ8f64S_ksQ@mail.gmail.com/T/#u#m49c2ecc7d787093a0fb97de67ccbedde0419eb51).

Further, the dialogue has included improvements in user experience for non-English speakers using Bitcoin wallets, suggesting a method for localized recovery experiences while maintaining interoperability with the English BIP39 standard. Initiatives like the integration of localized options in TZUR Wallet exemplify efforts to cater to a global audience, potentially enhancing security and user accessibility in the cryptocurrency tools. This community-driven approach underscores a commitment to inclusivity and functional enhancement of cryptocurrency infrastructures. [Read more about this discussion](https://gnusha.org/pi/bitcoindev/CAFS8eiVN2WmJi0gpRFR+ab_C6VOFi6oPBx-g_EULwMDvaF=EtQ@mail.gmail.com/T/#mb8f0ab5aa1e26808d26a0a9f19c23c29347883f1).

recent_posts.title: State of the transaction privacy work in Bitcoin
recent_posts.summary:
- The exploration of Bitcoin transaction privacy is essential in cryptocurrency discussions.
- A thread on DelvingBitcoin.org acts as a main resource for ongoing privacy challenges.
- Broader community participation is encouraged to enrich the dialogue and enhance privacy solutions.

recent_posts.title: Accountable Computing On-Chain Contracts for AI Agents Supervision
recent_posts.summary:
- Bitcoin development focuses on data annexes for P2TR transactions, enhancing AI supervision.
- Economic issues include the principal-agent problem, aligning AI incentives with user costs.
- Proposed protocol uses the Bitcoin blockchain for error cost management in AI responses.

recent_posts.title: Proposal discussion: BIP39 native-language display wordlists
recent_posts.summary: - The email discusses localization challenges in Bitcoin wallet recovery processes.  - It proposes a method where localized inputs map back to English BIP39 words.  - The sender's projects aim to improve user experience for non-English speakers.

recent_posts.title: Regular signet reorgs
recent_posts.summary:
- A strategy delays blockchain reorganizations until blocks are 25 minutes old.
- Transactions may be expelled from the mempool during reorgs, causing delays.
- This issue could affect transaction fee estimation and network reliability.

recent_posts.title: BIP39 native-language display wordlists mapped to canonical English
recent_posts.summary:
- Daniel proposes enhancements to BIP39 for non-English speakers' usability.
- The idea includes a native-language interface while keeping English as the core.
- He seeks community feedback to address potential risks and confusions.

recent_posts.title: Fountain Codes: a way to reduce blockchain storage costs
recent_posts.summary:
- The email outlines an innovative data recovery strategy using 1008 blocks and 100 anonymity groups.
- The strategy uses a storage reduction factor of 1/63, totaling about 11GB with 1600 droplets.
- It also discusses potential system adjustments during Initial Block Download to manage memory.

active_posts.title: [BIP Draft] P2P UTXO Set Sharing
active_posts.summary:
- The BIP updates UTXO set sharing to facilitate node bootstrapping and decentralization.
- New messages and a service bit streamline UTXO data transfer, improving setup times.
- Stakeholders can now review and comment on the BIP at the Bitcoin repository.

active_posts.title: Prohibit Merkle Internal Node Preimages That Encode Minimal 64-Byte Transactions
active_posts.summary: - The proposal introduces a new Bitcoin consensus rule for certain 64-byte transactions.  - It focuses on preventing security risks from SPV malleability by targeting specific transaction structures.  - Implementation advice includes a C++ function and guidelines for miners on block validation.

active_posts.title: Aligning privacy incentives in P2MR
active_posts.summary:
- The proposal modifies BIP360 to mandate a merkle path in P2MR transaction blocks.
- It aims to eliminate depth-zero script trees and enhance Bitcoin scripting privacy.
- Further feedback is sought, especially from P2TRv2 proponents, on its effectiveness.

active_posts.title: Public key recovery for EC leaves in P2MR (BIP-360)
active_posts.summary:
- The proposal improves BIP-360 by reducing EC witness size using a recoverable leaf.
- This method employs a modified Schnorr equation to recover and verify EC public keys.
- Changes in witness parsing and validation enhance security and efficiency in blockchain transactions.

active_posts.title: A Bitcoin-native LLM: dataset, architecture and open questions
active_posts.summary:
- The concept aims to enhance LLMs for specialized interaction with Bitcoin protocols.
- A substantial dataset from diverse Bitcoin contexts is crucial for model training.
- The architecture combines a base model and a tool-calling layer for diverse tasks.

active_posts.title: State of the transaction privacy work in Bitcoin
active_posts.summary:
- Transaction privacy in Bitcoin focuses on sidechains, L2s, and wallet integrations.
- Enhancements like CISA aim to reduce costs but do not increase privacy.
- The trend favors regulatory-compliant and less invasive privacy improvements.

today_in_history_posts.title: [PROPOSAL] OP_TX: generalized covenants reduced to OP_CHECKTEMPLATEVERIFY
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- On May 10, 2022, Rusty Russell discussed OPTX_SEPARATELY and OPTX_UNHASHED fields on bitcoin-dev.
- These methods were critiqued as less efficient than utilizing a merkle path.
- The discussion also highlighted concerns about upgradability with new features like CAT or TLUV.

today_in_history_posts.title: Achieving Zero Downtime Splicing in Practice via Chain Signals
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- Laolu Osuntokun proposed a splicing signal for the Lightning Network.
- Lisa Neigut questioned the proposal, stating it conflicts with anonymity goals.
- They considered adding the signal to enhance reliability despite potential issues.

today_in_history_posts.title: Reimagining Onion Messages as an Overlay Layer
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- Reimagining onion messaging with an overlay layer could enhance Lightning Network's efficiency.
- The overlay enables incremental deployment and improves user experience by reducing latency.
- New authentication protocols in the proposal mitigate risks and enable secure communication.
